what_it_does: Defines a single RELATION_TYPE dict for the 'part_of' board edge: a directed containment edge where a block/item is a part of a target container document (board://<document>), with inverse 'contains'.
observations: Has a module docstring explaining containment and how it pairs with an item's 'order' field.
Docstring states part_of gives membership while a separate 'order' field gives sequence.
Inverse 'contains' lets reverse_traverse find a document's blocks.
standouts: Explicit separation of concerns: membership (part_of edge) vs sequence (order field).
Has a real module docstring, more than the bare-dict siblings.
conventions: Adds a module-level docstring (richer than commented_on/composes_with/references which lack one).
notes: The block-to-document containment edge; membership via part_of, ordering left to a separate 'order' field.
questions: Where is the 'order' field defined on the document/block item type?
purpose_vs_actual: Match: docstring and desc both describe a part/block-of-container edge and the dict declares it.
intention_signals: Trying to model documents as containers of blocks via a containment edge, deliberately keeping ordering out of the edge so sequence is a separate concern.
novelty: The explicit membership/sequence split (part_of edge + order field) is the distinctive design choice here.
contribution: Contributes the containment edge with its 'contains' inverse and the documented membership-vs-sequence separation for assembling documents from blocks.
summary_for_embedding: A board relation-type definition for 'part_of', a directed containment edge (inverse contains) linking a block to its container document.
intention_for_embedding: To model documents as containers of member blocks, with membership expressed by the edge and sequence handled by a separate order field.
